message:
perf(btree,connection,vdbe): zero-traversal monotonic append with retained rightmost-leaf cache

Major performance improvement for sequential INSERT workloads by
eliminating redundant B-tree traversals on the hot append path.

B-tree cursor (fsqlite-btree):
- Enrich RightmostLeafCacheEntry with full page data, header, and cell
  pointers so the cached leaf image can be reused directly without
  re-reading from the pager
- Add TableAppendHint, an opaque retained append hint that callers can
  carry between cursor instances to keep the rightmost append path hot
- Add try_append_table_leaf_payload_in_place_no_overflow() for direct
  varint-encoded table leaf cell writes that skip overflow chain assembly
  when the payload fits entirely on the leaf page
- Add try_append_on_cached_rightmost_leaf() for zero-traversal append
  that writes directly into the cached leaf image without descending
  from the root or even pushing a stack entry
- Add try_table_append_on_hinted_leaf_with_known_last_rowid() and
  try_append_on_external_rightmost_leaf_hint() for external hint reuse
- Expose new public APIs: table_append_after_last_position(),
  table_try_append_rightmost_leaf_hint_known_last_rowid(),
  table_try_append_rightmost_leaf_hint_known_last_rowid_with_state(),
  table_try_append_cached_rightmost_leaf_hint(),
  table_cached_rightmost_leaf_hint(), swap_cell_scratch()
- Refactor try_append_leaf_page_in_place() out of the old monolithic
  append path so it can be called from multiple entry points
- Lazy cursor stack allocation (Vec::new() instead of
  Vec::with_capacity(BTREE_MAX_DEPTH)) since the hot append lane can
  complete without ever pushing a stack entry
- After page split during append, store the new rightmost leaf in cache
  instead of just clearing it, preserving the fast path for the next row
- Add last_known_depth tracking to avoid redundant tree depth scans
- Add is_on_rightmost_insert_edge() helper for position validation
- Comprehensive test coverage for all new append paths

Connection layer (fsqlite-core):
- Wire PreparedDirectInsertAppendHint to carry TableAppendHint state
  across executes so consecutive monotonic INSERTs skip cursor.last()
- Add prepared_direct_insert_cell_scratch buffer to preserve cell
  assembly allocation capacity across cursor lifetimes
- Add PREPARED_DIRECT_INSERT_PENDING_MEMDB_UPSERT_LIMIT constant
- Restructure execute_prepared_direct_simple_insert() to prefer cached
  rightmost leaf → known-last-rowid hint → hinted leaf → full insert

VDBE engine (fsqlite-vdbe):
- Inline root_page, rowid_mode, and autoincrement_high_water into
  StorageCursor to avoid repeated HashMap lookups on every NewRowid
  and Insert opcode execution
- Consolidate storage_cursor_autoincrement_high_water() and
  storage_cursor_rowid_mode() into a single storage_cursor_runtime_meta()
- Move concurrent rowid floor bump into the Insert handler's success
  path instead of a post-hoc lookup, eliminating a redundant
  cursor_root_pages probe
- Add table_append_after_last_position() dispatch to CursorBackend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
